compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Plan-review PASS (signed, 0 blocking, 10 advisory). Triage + concrete decisions:
- [E2 f2bb3b/f066be — what est_tokens counts + naming] The fit test is over the full verify REQUEST text = est_tokens(system_prompt_text + verify_instructions(chunk)); 'request' in the AC == that string. Use one name (request) in code+docs.
- [E2 f5f807 / single-oversized] 'too large' = est_tokens(request for a SINGLE finding) > budget. Escalation: models_at_or_above(resolved_verify_model); if even the top model's window can't fit it, mark that finding INDETERMINATE (non-blocking, surfaced) — never drop.
- [T2 fbc3cd] PER_FINDING_VERIFY_TOKENS default 256 is a documented, config-adjustable RESERVE (output scales per finding), not a derived constant; name it + document the basis (the plan_review_verification per-finding object size) and that it's tunable.
- [T4 f67b55] Rollout: the workflow already does ONE aggregate verify (bespoke's batch_size=12 was deleted in WS1), so this ADDS splitting only for >budget; the common case is byte-identical (one call). Back-out = the headroom knob / the splitting only triggers over budget.
- [E3 fcae03] Current verify-path callers: only the gate workflow (review_plan + drift probe via produce_plan_review_verdict) — both go through the same verify step, so chunking is single-site.
- [F1 ff3916 / E3 fb8f28] The >budget test uses a deterministic window-shrink seam (shrink largest_window_tokens in the test) so a small findings set exceeds budget → asserts principled splitting (N>1 calls, merged by index), NOT count-12; and asserts est+headroom keeps the per-chunk request under the (shrunk) window.
- [E1 fbda4a / F4 f29fb9] AC-7 (make lint/test) is a standard hygiene gate; beneficiary = an operator/agent running plan-review on a pathological huge-findings ticket (verify no longer blows the context window). Both noted, no change needed.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
WS3 approach decided via research (Joe's directive — what actively-maintained projects do): OPTION 1 — encapsulate chunk+merge INSIDE the verify step, the mainstream LLM-framework pattern (LangChain MapReduceDocumentsChain, LlamaIndex map_reduce/tree_summarize all hide chunking inside the operation; LangGraph Send+reducer and Dagster .collect()/Beam Combine are the heavier workflow-level fan-in primitives — gold standard for general engines but more machinery). Consensus: with 1M-token windows chunking is rarely triggered, so the COMMON case stays ONE aggregate call (no behavior change); chunking is a rare safety valve.
Concrete design for our engine: (1) a sizing helper computes global-index-preserving, token-budgeted chunks of the findings (est_tokens(system+instructions)+per-finding output reserve vs floor(largest_window_tokens(model)*headroom)); (2) verify_inputs emits instruction_chunks (a LIST; 1 element in the common case); (3) RunnerAgentStep gains GENERIC support: when a prompt step's instructions input is a list, it runs the prompt once per chunk and MERGES the structured output by concatenating list-valued fields (verifications) — encapsulated in the step, like LangChain. Single-oversized finding that can't fit even the top model's window is simply NOT verified → pass3_decide(None) already routes it to INDETERMINATE (free, no special path). Refs: LangChain MapReduceDocumentsChain; LlamaIndex response synthesizers (map_reduce, tree_summarize); LangGraph Send API + Annotated[list, operator.add] reducer; Dagster .collect(); AWS Step Functions Map array-aggregation.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

The original epic problem: the plan-review verify pass chunks findings token-naively. Bespoke used a
hard-coded `batch_size=12` (`range(0, len(findings), 12)`); the workflow does ONE aggregate call, which
a pathological huge-findings ticket could push past the model context window. Replace the magic count
with PRINCIPLED, token-based chunking — estimate the verify request vs the selected model's window
(reusing the `sizing.py` machinery), splitting only when needed. This depends on WS1 (single verify
path) and WS2 (the per-step `model:` whose window we size against).

## What

- Replace any count-based batching with a token estimate vs the verify step's resolved model window,
  reusing `largest_window_tokens(model)` + the `est_tokens` (chars/4) estimator — no new magic constant,
  no new tokenizer dependency.
- Budget formula: `budget = floor(largest_window_tokens(model) * HEADROOM)`, `HEADROOM` a named config
  knob `verify.verify_window_headroom` (default 0.8). A chunk fits when
  `est_tokens(system_prompt + verify_instructions(chunk)) + projected_output(chunk) <= budget`.
- Projected output: `projected_output(chunk) = len(chunk) * PER_FINDING_VERIFY_TOKENS`, a named
  per-finding reserve sized from the `plan_review_verification` schema (~256 tok/finding, documented).
- `<= budget` → ONE aggregate verify call (unchanged). `> budget` → minimal principled splits that
  preserve GLOBAL finding enumeration and re-merge by finding `index` (existing merge contract).
- Single-oversized-finding edge case: escalate from the RESOLVED verify model via
  `models_at_or_above(resolved_verify_model)`; if still too large at the top of the ladder, mark the
  finding INDETERMINATE (non-blocking, surfaced) — never silently drop.

## Scope

In scope: the token-budget chunking decision in the workflow verify path, the `verify.verify_window_headroom`
config key, the `PER_FINDING_VERIFY_TOKENS` reserve, the oversized-finding escalation/INDETERMINATE
path, and tests (via a deterministic window-shrink seam). Out of scope: WS1 migration, WS2 model/editor
work, WS4 coach nit. Depends on WS1 (single path) and WS2 (resolved per-step model). Touches
`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sizing.py`, `workflow_ops.py` (the verify-inputs op), and config.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A token estimate (no magic `batch_size` constant) decides verify chunking, reusing
      `largest_window_tokens` + `est_tokens`.
- [ ] The fit test is `est_tokens(request) + projected_output <= floor(window * HEADROOM)`, where
      `HEADROOM` is the config knob `verify.verify_window_headroom` (default 0.8) and
      `projected_output = len(chunk) * PER_FINDING_VERIFY_TOKENS` (a named per-finding reserve).
- [ ] `<= budget` findings → one aggregate verify call (unchanged behavior).
- [ ] `> budget` findings → minimal principled splits that preserve global enumeration and re-merge
      verifications by finding `index`; a test (via a deterministic window-shrink seam) asserts
      principled splitting, NOT count-12.
- [ ] The `> budget` test also asserts the chars/4 estimate plus headroom keeps a real request under
      the actual model window (the estimator safety margin is grounded, not assumed).
- [ ] A single finding larger than the budget escalates from the resolved verify model via
      `models_at_or_above`; if still too large at the top of the ladder it is marked INDETERMINATE
      (non-blocking, surfaced) — never silently dropped; a test covers this path.
- [ ] `make lint` + `make check` + the full test suite pass.

file_impact.reason: chunk verify inputs by token budget

compiled_state.title: WS3: Principled token-based chunking for the workflow Pass-2 verify
